title: Is Apple Prepared for a Succession?
author: Blane Warrene (reporter, MacNewsWorld); Peter Kastner, Chad Mader (quoted sources)
abstract: MacNewsWorld analysis of Apple's succession planning after Steve Jobs's pancreatic cancer disclosure and 30-day medical leave (August 2004). Peter Kastner, executive vice president at Aberdeen Group, plays down near-term governance risk — the head of sales is running the company during back-to-school season — but the article broadly raises concern about Apple's lack of disclosed succession plan.
importance_rationale: Captures Kastner's 2004 governance read of Apple at the moment of Jobs's cancer disclosure; an important inflection point in Apple governance history.
relevance_rationale: The succession question ultimately resolved with Tim Cook (2011), and Apple governance has since matured; article now serves as a historical record of how analysts parsed the 2004 scare.
prescience_rationale: Kastner's 'no major management issues from 30-day leave' read was accurate for the immediate 2004 event, but he did not anticipate the much larger 2009 liver-transplant leave or 2011 succession. Short-term call: correct. Long-horizon succession: not addressed.
